pattern: Sorting lets a greedy sweep open a fresh group only when the current element drifts more than k from the group's smallest member.
hints: Since subsequences ignore original order, you may sort the values freely before grouping.
After sorting, a group can stretch from its smallest element up to that value plus k; extend greedily and start a new group when you exceed it.
Sort, set the first group's anchor to the smallest value, and scan: whenever the current value exceeds anchor plus k, increment the count and reset the anchor to the current value.

edge_inputs.description: k zero forces a group per distinct value
all identical values fit one group
range exactly k fits in one group
range one beyond k needs two groups

stress.description: large array with random values and a moderate k
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 5000))
    nums = [rng.randrange(0, 100001) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randrange(0, 1001)
    return [nums, k]
